steps.instruction: Wash your hands before touching the wound.
Rinse the cut with clean running water to remove dirt and debris.
Apply gentle pressure with clean gauze until bleeding stops.
Cover the area with a sterile bandage and change it daily.

topics.name: Deeper Cuts
topics.description: Cuts that may need extra care

steps.instruction: Apply firm pressure with a clean cloth or gauze.
Elevate the injured area if possible.
If bleeding does not stop after 10 minutes, seek urgent medical care.
Cool the burn under cool running water for 20 minutes.
Remove rings or tight items before swelling starts.
Cover loosely with a clean non-stick dressing.
Call emergency services for large or deep burns.
Do not apply ice, butter, or ointments to the burned area.
Cover with a clean cloth and monitor for shock.

topics.name: Ankle Sprain
topics.description: Swelling or pain after twisting the ankle

steps.instruction: Rest the ankle and avoid putting weight on it.
Apply ice wrapped in cloth for 15 to 20 minutes at a time.
Use a compression wrap if it does not cause extra pain.
Elevate the foot above heart level when possible.

topics.name: Wrist Sprain
topics.description: Pain and tenderness after a fall or strain

steps.instruction: Stop using the wrist and rest it in a comfortable position.
Apply ice and a light compression bandage.
Seek medical assessment if the wrist looks deformed or pain is severe.

topics.name: Adult Choking
topics.description: A conscious adult unable to cough or speak

steps.instruction: Ask if the person can speak or cough.
If they cannot breathe, give five back blows between the shoulder blades.
Follow with five abdominal thrusts and repeat until the object clears.
Call emergency services if the airway does not clear quickly.
Keep the child calm and check if they can cough.
Give age-appropriate back blows and thrusts if the airway is blocked.
Call emergency services immediately if breathing is impaired.
Drink plenty of fluids and rest.
Wear light clothing and keep the room comfortably cool.
Use fever medicine only as directed on the label or by a clinician.
Seek care for trouble breathing, confusion, or a stiff neck.
Get help for fever that lasts several days or keeps returning.
Watch closely for dehydration, especially in children and older adults.

It was caused by something rusty or dirty

Questions.question: When should you seek medical care for burns?
answers: The burn affects the face or respiratory tract
The victim has a third degree burn
The burn affects more than one body part or more than 15% of the body

Questions.question: How should you treat mild burns?
answers: Flush the area with large amounts of cool water
Questions.correctAnswer: Flush the area with large amounts of cool water

Questions.question: What should you do after cooling and cleaning a burn?
answers: Apply a non-stick dressing to the burn site
Questions.correctAnswer: Apply a non-stick dressing to the burn site

Questions.question: How should you treat blisters on a burn?
answers: Do not break the blisters and wrap it in a sterile non-stick dressing
Questions.correctAnswer: Do not break the blisters and wrap it in a sterile non-stick dressing

Questions.question: What are the signs of a 1st degree burn?
answers: Only the top layer of skin is affected
Redness, dry skin, or skin peeling

Questions.question: What are the signs of a second degree burn?
answers: The burn and skin look wet, shiny, or moist
There is significant swelling around the burned area

Questions.question: What are the signs of a third degree burn?
answers: Dry and charred skin, the area feeling very painful or not at all, and significant swelling
Not very painful, skin is red and shiny or moist
Questions.correctAnswer: Dry and charred skin, the area feeling very painful or not at all, and significant swelling

Questions.question: What are the care steps for a severe burn?
answers: Call 911 immediately and do not remove clothing stuck to the burn
Apply cool, not cold, water for five minutes then gently dry
If you can, elevate the burn above the heart and cover with a dressing
All of the above, in this order
Questions.correctAnswer: All of the above, in this order

Questions.question: What are the care steps for a severe burn?
answers: Call 911 immediately and do not remove clothing stuck to the burn
Apply cool, not cold, water for five minutes then gently dry
If you can, elevate the burn above the heart and cover with a dressing
All of the above, in this order
Questions.correctAnswer: All of the above, in this order

Questions.question: What are signs of complete choking?
answers: Being unable to cough, speak, or breath
Skin that becomes bluish or paler than normal

Questions.question: How can you reduce the risk of choking?
answers: Avoid talking and physical activity while chewing

Questions.question: How can you tell if someone is partially choking but not completely choking?
answers: One or both hands are around their throat
If they are coughing, wheezing, or gasping
Questions.correctAnswer: If they are coughing, wheezing, or gasping

Questions.question: Why should someone who has suffered complete choking always seek medical attention?
answers: The lodged object could have damaged the airway
Back blows or abdominal thrusts could have caused damage
